channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Musk says failure to respond to email a second time will end in termination
item.description: The message appears to contradict recent guidance from the Office of Personnel Management (OPM), which told agency leaders Monday afternoon that employee response to the initial email was not mandatory and that failure to do so would not be considered a resignation.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about air traffic control towers after a midair collision in Arizona prompts questions
item.description: Tens of thousands of planes take off, land and perform touch-and-goes at the Marana Regional Airport in southern Arizona every year. Without an air traffic control tower, it's a calculated dance that requires communication by pilots.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Illinois landlord charged with 2023 hate crime and murder of Palestinian American boy set for trial
item.description: An Illinois landlord charged with murder, attempted murder and a hate crime in a 2023 attack on a Palestinian American mother and her young son is expected at trial.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Tareen, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Catholic community in Utah 'united in prayer' for the health of Pope Francis
item.description: The pope showed some slight improvements in recent tests, and as he continues to recover, members of the Catholic faith around the world — including in Utah — continue to pray for his health.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Antivirus software company ordered to pay customer $16.5M: How to file a claim
item.description: Millions of American consumers who bought antivirus software from Avast may be eligible to receive a portion of a $16.5 million settlement, the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: Funeral set for an officer killed while responding to a gunman in a Pennsylvania hospital
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A funeral service will be held later this week for a Pennsylvania police officer shot and killed while responding to a hospital intensive care unit where authorities said a gunman had shot three hospital employees and taken staff members hostage last weekend.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: List: Bay Area JOANN stores closing part of more than 500 shutting down nationwide
item.description: NEW YORK (AP/KRON) — Fabric and crafts retailer Joann Inc., which has been a destination for generations of quilters, knitters and lovers of crafts projects for more than 80 years, is going out of business and shuttering all its stores.
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Police: Holland mom said she killed son to fulfill his death wish
item.description: A mom accused of stabbing her son to death told police she was helping him fulfill his wish: to die before his 18th birthday.
item.dc:creator: Ken Kolker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Clint Hill, Secret Service agent who leaped onto JFK's car after the president was shot, dies at 93
item.description: Clint Hill received Secret Service awards and was promoted for his actions that day, but for decades blamed himself for Kennedy's death.
item.dc:creator: Tom Palmer, NewsNation

item.title: American detained in Paris after baby is allegedly thrown from hotel window
item.description: The American was taken to a hospital for a post-delivery operation where she was placed in custody, according to the Paris Prosecutor’s Office.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Delta flight returns to Atlanta after possible smoke reported in flightdeck
item.description: A Delta flight returned to Atlanta on Monday after the crew observed a “haze” in the aircraft shortly after takeoff, the airline said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge rejects immediately restoring AP's access to White House but urges government to reconsider
item.description: A federal judge has refused to immediately order the White House to restore The Associated Press’ access to presidential events, saying the news organization had not shown it had suffered irreparable harm in the matter.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sedensky, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump defends Musk’s ‘genius’ email to federal workers
item.description: President Trump on Monday defended tech billionaire Elon Musk after pushback over his email demanding federal workers list five accomplishments from the past week and in doing so, raising the of risk losing their jobs. “There was a lot of genius in sending it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Watch: Trump, Macron hold White House press conference
item.description: President Donald Trump hosts French President Emmanuel Macron on Monday, the first visit by a European leader to the White House since Trump returned.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, KRON4

item.title: Starbucks lays off 1,100 corporate employees as coffee chain streamlines
item.description: Starbucks plans to lay off 1,100 corporate employees globally as new Chairman and CEO Brian Niccol streamlines operations. In a letter to employees released Monday, Niccol said the company will inform employees who are being laid off by midday Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: After 78 years, Texas church wins mineral royalties battle
item.description: "To finally figure out that they have stopped, you know, paying the wrong people for church minerals is a joyous day for us,” Church historian Johnathan Matthews said.
item.dc:creator: Ashlyn Anderson, NewsNation

item.title: Federal workers confront mass confusion as Musk's deadline looms
item.description: Confusion and chaos loom as hundreds of thousands of federal employees begin their workweek facing a deadline from Elon Musk to explain their recent accomplishments or risk losing their jobs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Russia-Ukraine war enters third year
item.description: As the war in Ukraine enters its third year, the United States is ramping up efforts to broker a peace deal, but sharp divisions are emerging over how to approach Russia’s role in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, KRON4

item.title: Trump announces Dan Bongino as Deputy FBI Director
item.description: Bongino is a former U.S. Secret Service agent that became known for his defense of Trump while previously appearing as a Fox News contributor.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Jury selection begins for trial of suburban Chicago July 4 parade mass shooting suspect
item.description: Jury selection is underway for the trial of the man accused of opening fire on a suburban Chicago Independence Day parade and killing seven people in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Tareen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope Francis in critical condition with early kidney failure but remains alert as prayers pour in
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Sunday and blood tests showed early kidney failure but he remains alert, responsive and attended Mass, the Vatican said, as the 88-year-old pontiff battles pneumonia and a complex lung infection.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Bell takes only lead in OT to edge Hocevar, Larson in another thrilling NASCAR finish in Atlanta
item.description: Christopher Bell celebrated a drought-ending victory for himself and Joe Gibbs Racing after a dramatic comeback in Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The Trump administration is firing 2,000 USAID workers and putting thousands of others on leave
item.description: The Trump administration said Sunday that it was placing all but a fraction of staffers at the U.S. Agency for International Development on leave worldwide and eliminating 2,000 U.S.-based staff positions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Massive brawl erupts at Atlanta airport terminal, viral video shows
item.description: Several travelers were caught on camera tackling one another in a massive brawl that broke out at one of the world's busiest airports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Luxury portable toilets sought after ahead of NFL Draft
item.description: The luxury restroom trailers and containers feature flushing toilets, sinks, air conditioning, and even stereo systems.
item.dc:creator: Timothy Littau, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis remains in critical condition with early kidney failure
item.description: Pope Francis was conscious but still receiving high flows of supplemental oxygen Sunday following a respiratory crisis and blood transfusions, as he remains in critical condition with a complex lung infection, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mikaela Shiffrin gets historic 100th World Cup race win and ties record for most podiums
item.description: Shiffrin’s record-extending 100th career World Cup race win Sunday fulfilled a quest put on hold by a serious crash in November.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Beware of text messages about unpaid tolls, FBI and state authorities warn
item.description: Drivers should be on the lookout for scam text messages warning of an unpaid road toll bill, according to recent warnings from authorities.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: One dead, one injured after shooting at New Mexico Air Force base
item.description: Kirtland Air Force base said the 377th Security Forces Squadron was called to an incident at approximately 2 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump DEI executive orders
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked parts of President Trump’s executive orders that sought to end the federal government’s backing of diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) efforts.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pennsylvania Gov. Shapiro meets with family of fallen officer, victims of hospital shooting
item.description: "I am inspired by the amazing doctors, nurses, and staff at UPMC and WellSpan Health who did tremendous work to save lives in the face of tragedy," said Shapiro. "It was my honor to meet with them and to thank them for what they do in service to their neighbors.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, WGN 9

item.title: Trump-Putin summit preparations are underway, Russia says
item.description: Preparations are underway for a face-to-face meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin, Russia's deputy foreign minister said Saturday. It marked a clear departure from Western efforts to isolate Moscow over its war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Delta faces first lawsuits days after plane flips in Toronto
item.description: All 21 people injured in the crash landing have been released from the hospital, and no one was killed.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5
